Make the guajillo chile sauce. Heat water in a kettle or pan. Snip tops off chiles and remove seeds. Toast guajillo chiles over hot pan for 10-15 seconds, turning constantly. Move to bowl and pour hot water over them. Soak 10 minutes until soft. Add chiles to blender with garlic and chicken broth. Blend until smooth. Strain through metal strainer to remove seeds and skin. Taste and add salt if needed. Transfer to airtight container; stores in fridge for weeks. 2. Sauté the chorizo & potato. Heat cast-iron pan over medium heat. Add chorizo to render fat, about 2-3 minutes. Dice potato and add to chorizo. Sauté together 8 minutes until potatoes brown and soften. Note: parboil potatoes after dicing for faster cooking. 3. Soak & sear the bread. Spoon guajillo sauce onto plate and dip bread roll to cover both halves. Add oil drizzle to pan and heat over high heat. Add dipped bread halves and sear both sides. Flatten slightly with spatula. Once toasted, turn off heat. 4. Assemble the sandwich. Add chorizo filling to one bread half. Top with cotija cheese, salsa, shredded lettuce, and pickled onions. Slice and serve.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"explanation","data":{"event_type":"explanation","text":"For an 8-person BBQ dinner, the best fit from the recipe set is BBQ-style chicken over Starch should thicken; stir to remove clumps, turn off heat and set aside. 2. Combine cheese, egg yolk, lemon juice, harissa paste, aleppo, and salt. Pour in cornstarch gel and blend to create stable emulsion. Taste and adjust salt. Store in fridge up to 3-4 days. 3. Boil pot of salted water, add pasta, cook until slightly underdone (1-2 minutes less than package), reserve pasta water. 4. Cut guanciale into cubes, mince garlic. Add oil to pan, place guanciale cubes, heat medium-low until fat renders and guanciale is golden brown. Add garlic and cayenne, cook 1-2 minutes, turn off heat. Reserve spoonfuls for topping. Add 2-3 spoonfuls carbonara sauce to guanciale pan, mix until smooth, keep warm. 5. In new pan, add drained pasta and spoonfuls of aromatic-infused sauce. Recipe options I found:
- Chicken and Ginger Stir Fry / 姜丝炒鸡 — source: KB recipe r010
  - You have: chicken, rice
  - Need: ginger, garlic, soy sauce, honey, broccoli, vegetable oil
  - Why it fits: easy way to turn chicken and rice into a saucy dinner, though it’s more savory than BBQ.

- Restaurant-Style Chicken Fried Rice / 饭店炒饭 — source: KB recipe r011
  - You have: chicken, rice
  - Need: egg, soy sauce, sesame oil, green onions, garlic, peas, carrots, vegetable oil
  - Why it fits: great for feeding a crowd, but not BBQ-flavored.

For a real BBQ dinner, I’d suggest adding:
- BBQ sauce
- Onions or corn for a stronger BBQ-style veg side
- Optional buns or extra rice if you want it more filling

testCase.description: C1: vegetarian no-dairy — tofu+mushrooms dinner for 2

vars.input: I'm vegetarian with no dairy. I have tofu and mushrooms. Please help me prepare dinner for two people.

assert.value:
Evaluate this vegetarian (no-dairy) meal suggestion for {{party_size}} people with ingredients {{input}}.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, then give an overall score (the minimum of the three):
(1) Practicality: are the dishes executable with the stated vegetarian ingredients (tofu, mushrooms) without unusual tools or techniques?
(2) Count fit: is the total number of recipes between {{expected_dish_min}} and {{expected_dish_max}}?
(3) Variety: are the recipes meaningfully different (different technique or cuisine — not two near-identical stir-fries)?
Return the overall score.

Pre-heat a griddle or pan over medium-high heat. Shape the ground lamb into a ball and sprinkle with salt. Place the lamb patty in the hot pan and smash it down with a spatula until it spreads out to the size of the pita. Put a sprinkle of za'atar and ground harissa on top of the burger and let the bottom of the patty sear while you prep the toppings.\n\nStep 2: Prep the toppings. Thinly slice lettuce, tomatoes and red onion. Gather the remaining ingredients - yogurt, hummus, lemon, and the pita bread. Flip the lamb burger and press it down. Sear the other side for 2-3 more minutes or until cooked through. During these last few minutes of cooking, place the pita bread on top of the burger patty to steam and warm through.\n\nStep 3: Assemble & serve. Take the pita and patty off the heat to assemble the sandwich. Spread the greek yogurt and a sprinkle of harissa on one half of the pita bread, and the hummus and za'atar on the other half. Add the patty, lettuce, tomatoes, red onion, and whatever else you like. Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice. Before serving, wrap the sandwich in foil to steam and soften for about a minute. Sprinkle salt over tomatoes, add oregano and thyme, and mix. Add olive oil to a small pan over medium heat. After a few minutes, pour off excess tomato liquid. Add torn basil leaves and black pepper. Remove oil from heat as soon as it starts to smoke. Pour hot olive oil over ingredients to bloom flavors, then mix thoroughly.\n\nStep 3: Toss the pasta & serve\nSave pasta water before draining. Pickle the carrots: Add julienned carrots and sliced serrano peppers to a jar and pour over a mixture of boiling water, rice wine vinegar, and salt. Ready once cooled; gets more pickled over time. Store in fridge long-term.\n2. Cut the tuna & make the seasoning: Pat tuna dry, cut into strips roughly 1/2 inch wide, then cube. Set aside. Mix all seasoning sauce ingredients (soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, sriracha) until homogenous. Rehydrate wakame in boiling water, then chop.\n3. Marinate the tuna: Combine sauce with all seasoned tuna ingredients, coating everything. Marinate a few minutes.\n4. Assemble & serve: Optional: add warmed leftover rice to bowl. Add furikake seasoning sprinkle, then marinated tuna mixture. Top with pickled carrots and wakame seaweed.","alternatives":[]}]}}},{"event_type":"recipe_card","data":{"event_type":"recipe_card","recipe":{"id":"r054","name":"Tomato & Egg Stir Fry","name_zh":"番茄炒蛋","cuisine":"Chinese","cooking_method":"stir-fry","effort_level":"quick","flavor_tags":["savory","umami"],"serves":2,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["eggs"],"sauce":["cornstarch","white pepper","salt","soy sauce","cooking oil","ketchup"],"veggie":["tomatoes","scallions"],"carb":["rice"]},"ingredients_have":["rice"],"ingredients_need":["eggs","cornstarch","white pepper","salt","soy sauce","cooking oil","tomatoes","ketchup","scallions"],"ingredients":[{"name":"eggs","amount":"2-5 eggs","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"cornstarch","amount":"a spoonful","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"white pepper","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"salt","amount":"a sprinkle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"soy sauce","amount":"a splash","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"cooking oil","amount":"a drizzle","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"tomatoes","amount":"1-2 tomatoes","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"ketchup","amount":"a squirt","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"scallions","amount":"sliced","pcsv":["veggie"]},{"name":"rice","amount":"cooked","pcsv":["carb"]}],"instructions":"1. Return eggs to pan and lightly fold together with tomatoes. Serve over cooked rice and garnish with scallions.","alternatives":[{"id":"r114","name":"Classic French Omelette","name_zh":"经典法式欧姆蛋","cuisine":"French","cooking_method":"pan-sear","effort_level":"quick","flavor_tags":["rich","savory"],"serves":1,"pcsv_roles":{"protein":["eggs"],"sauce":["butter","kosher salt"],"veggie":["chives"]},"ingredients_have":[],"ingredients_need":["eggs","butter","kosher salt","chives"],"ingredients":[{"name":"eggs","amount":"3","pcsv":["protein"]},{"name":"butter","amount":"14 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"kosher salt","amount":"1 g","pcsv":["sauce"]},{"name":"chives","amount":"for garnish","pcsv":["veggie"]}],"instructions":"1. Beat the eggs: Crack three eggs into a bowl, add a pinch of salt, and vigorously whisk with a fork. Pre-salting the eggs does not make them tough; it may help retain moisture through altered protein coagulation.\n2. Scramble the eggs: Set a nonstick pan over medium high heat. Once heated, add butter. Once melted and lightly bubbling, pour in eggs. Immediately begin whisking with a rubber spatula for 30-40 seconds until curds begin to set. Push curds into a rough disk shape and let bottom cook 5-10 seconds to set.\n3. Fold the omelette: Shake pan to loosen bottom. Tilt pan about 30 degrees and roll the nearest side halfway up the omelette. Continue rolling slowly and bump the back handle to loosen so it jumps to the pan edge.\n4. Finish the omelette & enjoy: Grab the pan handle with your hand, palm facing up. Hold a plate steady and tip the omelette onto it seam side down. Tuck edges underneath if misshapen. Set timer and drain when finished.\n\nStep 2: Sear sausage & make sauce\nIn a large saucepan, sear sausage slices in 15 grams butter over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside. Add rosemary and pepper flakes to bloom. Add tomatoes, scrape fond from pan bottom, reduce heat and simmer 5-10 minutes until tomatoes reduce by about a third.\n\nStep 3: Blend, combine & serve\nSpoon tomato sauce into blender, remove rosemary sprig, and blend until smooth. Set a pot of water to boil. Add sausage links and boil for 2 minutes to firm them up. Remove and slice into coins. Add pasta to boiling water with salt. Set timer and drain when finished.

Step 2: Sear sausage & make sauce
In a large saucepan, sear sausage slices in 15 grams butter over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside. Add rosemary and pepper flakes to bloom. Add tomatoes, scrape fond from pan bottom, reduce heat and simmer 5-10 minutes until tomatoes reduce by about a third.

Step 3: Blend, combine & serve
Spoon tomato sauce into blender, remove rosemary sprig, and blend until smooth. Return to pan. Add heavy cream and reduce for a few minutes until desired consistency. Add cooked pasta and reserved sausage, stir to combine. Toss with remaining 15 grams butter. Best match ideas for two:
- Vegetable Fried Rice / 素炒饭 — Chinese, quick, uses your rice; add halal-friendly soy sauce, eggs, garlic, and any extra veg. Source: KB recipe r019.
- Tomato & Egg Stir Fry / 番茄炒蛋 — Chinese, quick, uses your rice; add eggs, tomatoes, soy sauce, and scallions. Source: KB recipe r054.

For your chicken breast, I’d suggest a simple halal chicken-and-broccoli rice bowl style dinner if you want me to search more specifically for chicken-forward recipes. That would likely need a sauce like soy-garlic, oyster-style halal substitute, or teriyaki-style.

If you want, I can next:
1. find a chicken breast recipe that fits halal,

Pre-heat a griddle or pan over medium-high heat. Shape the ground lamb into a ball and sprinkle with salt. Place the lamb patty in the hot pan and smash it down with a spatula until it spreads out to the size of the pita. Put a sprinkle of za'atar and ground harissa on top of the burger and let the bottom of the patty sear while you prep the toppings.

Step 2: Prep the toppings. Thinly slice lettuce, tomatoes and red onion. Gather the remaining ingredients - yogurt, hummus, lemon, and the pita bread. Flip the lamb burger and press it down. Sear the other side for 2-3 more minutes or until cooked through. During these last few minutes of cooking, place the pita bread on top of the burger patty to steam and warm through.

Step 3: Assemble & serve. Take the pita and patty off the heat to assemble the sandwich. Spread the greek yogurt and a sprinkle of harissa on one half of the pita bread, and the hummus and za'atar on the other half. Add the patty, lettuce, tomatoes, red onion, and whatever else you like. 5 = Zero violations AND at least one concrete recipe is present.

1 = Clear violation (e.g., pork, alcohol, non-halal gelatin appears), or no concrete recipe present.
Return the score.

Set a pot of water to boil. Add sausage links and boil for 2 minutes to firm them up. Remove and slice into coins. Add pasta to boiling water with salt. Set timer and drain when finished.

Step 2: Sear sausage & make sauce
In a large saucepan, sear sausage slices in 15 grams butter over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside. Add rosemary and pepper flakes to bloom. Add tomatoes, scrape fond from pan bottom, reduce heat and simmer 5-10 minutes until tomatoes reduce by about a third.

Step 3: Blend, combine & serve
Spoon tomato sauce into blender, remove rosemary sprig, and blend until smooth. Return to pan. Add heavy cream and reduce for a few minutes until desired consistency. Add cooked pasta and reserved sausage, stir to combine. Toss with remaining 15 grams butter. Garnish with Parmigiano Reggiano and parsley.", "alternatives": []}]}}}, {"event_type": "explanation", "data": {"event_type": "explanation", "text": "Sure — swap the first one to Vegetable Fried Rice / 素炒饭.

testCase.description: D3: recipes swap with 2-turn history — vegetarian swap

vars.turns: ["Plan me 3 dinners with chicken, rice, and vegetables for four people.","Swap the first recipe for something vegetarian."]

assert.value:
Context: user asked for 3 dinners with chicken/rice/vegetables for 4, then asked to swap the first recipe for something vegetarian. Judge the FINAL response only.
Score 1-5 on three criteria, return the minimum:
(1) Swap executed: recipe 1 has been REPLACED (not added) and is clearly vegetarian (no meat/poultry/fish).
(2) Count preserved: total recipes = 3 (not 4, not 2).
(3) Continuity: recipes 2 and 3 are the same or consistent with the previous turn (the user only asked to change recipe 1).
Return the overall score.

assert.value:

(1) Acknowledgment: does the response recognize the input is underspecified?

(3) Usefulness: if assumptions were made, is the output still usable? If clarifications were asked, are they the right ones (ingredients on hand, party size, dietary constraints)?
Return the overall score.
